channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Questioning Georgia O'Keefe's legacy and protecting the land in northern New Mexico
item.description: Georgia O'Keeffe claimed New Mexico's desert "my country." But Pueblo peoples lived on the land long before the artist arrived. There's a push for a more complex view of O'Keeffe and her time there.
item.content:encoded: Georgia O'Keeffe claimed New Mexico's desert "my country." But Pueblo peoples lived on the land long before the artist arrived. There's a push for a more complex view of O'Keeffe and her time there.
item.dc:creator: John Burnett

item.title: Teachers across California are considering work stoppages
item.description: Teachers across California have been preparing for potential strikes as state funding for schools struggles to keep up with costs.
item.content:encoded: Teachers across California have been preparing for potential strikes as state funding for schools struggles to keep up with costs.
item.dc:creator: Farida Romero

item.title: Pakistan is trying to straddle warring sides as conflict widens
item.description: Pakistan faces a bind as the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran widens. It is trying to straddle warring sides, while managing public frustration at home.
item.content:encoded: Pakistan faces a bind as the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran widens. It is trying to straddle warring sides, while managing public frustration at home.
item.dc:creator: Betsy Joles

item.title: 'Peaky Blinders' is on the big screen in 'The Immortal Man'
item.description: After six seasons of streaming, Cillian Murphy's Tommy Shelby brings his Romani family of Birmingham gangsters to the big screen.
item.content:encoded: After six seasons of streaming, Cillian Murphy's Tommy Shelby brings his Romani family of Birmingham gangsters to the big screen.
item.dc:creator: Bob Mondello

item.title: Political and religious leaders celebrate Jesse Jackson's life at 'homegoing' service
item.description: A celebration of the life of civil rights leader Jesse Jackson will be held in Chicago Friday. Current and former elected officials, faith leaders, entertainers and more are expected to attend. A private funeral will be held Saturday.
item.content:encoded: A celebration of the life of civil rights leader Jesse Jackson will be held in Chicago Friday. Current and former elected officials, faith leaders, entertainers and more are expected to attend. A private funeral will be held Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Cheryl Corley

item.title: As the war goes on in Iran, American-Iranians struggle to stay in touch with family
item.description: Many Iranians in the U.S. with family back in Iran have been having a very difficult time staying in touch as bombs continue to drop around the country. That's creating a lot of anxiety and worry.
item.content:encoded: Many Iranians in the U.S. with family back in Iran have been having a very difficult time staying in touch as bombs continue to drop around the country. That's creating a lot of anxiety and worry.
item.dc:creator: Kelly McEvers

item.title: In one week, war has swept across the Middle East
item.description: One week into the U.S.–Israeli bombing campaign against Iran, the conflict is already spilling far beyond its original battlefield — with shockwaves reaching from Cyprus to Sri Lanka.
item.content:encoded: One week into the U.S.–Israeli bombing campaign against Iran, the conflict is already spilling far beyond its original battlefield — with shockwaves reaching from Cyprus to Sri Lanka.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Kahn

item.title: In one week, war has swept across the Middle East
item.description: Nearly a week into a new and widening war in the Middle East, some of the U.S.'s closest allies in the region and beyond are trying to contain it.
item.content:encoded: Nearly a week into a new and widening war in the Middle East, some of the U.S.'s closest allies in the region and beyond are trying to contain it.
item.dc:creator: Juana Summers

item.title: How a handful of koalas are changing scientists understanding of genetic risk
item.description: It's long been assumed that koalas in southern Australia are genetically unhealthy. A new study finds they're actually recovering, changing how scientists look at genetic risks.
item.content:encoded: It's long been assumed that koalas in southern Australia are genetically unhealthy. A new study finds they're actually recovering, changing how scientists look at genetic risks.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: Advocate for military families on what wartime deployment means at home
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Kathy Roth-Douquet, CEO of Blue Star Families, about the challenges that military families face around deployment.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Kathy Roth-Douquet, CEO of Blue Star Families, about the challenges that military families face around deployment.
item.dc:creator: Vincent Acovino
